findings.evidence: Grep hits: `mathlib/Mathlib/GroupTheory/Perm/Support.lean:221 lemma set_support_symm_eq : {x | p.symm x ≠ x} = {x | p x ≠ x}` and `:235 theorem set_support_mul_subset : { x | (p * q) x ≠ x } ⊆ { x | p x ≠ x } ∪ { x | q x ≠ x }`; `mathlib/Mathlib/GroupTheory/Perm/ClosureSwap.lean:64 theorem finite_compl_fixedBy_swap` covers the transposition support finiteness.
findings.fix: Import/reuse `Mathlib.GroupTheory.Perm.Support` (and `ClosureSwap` for the swap case if desired) and derive these lemmas from `Equiv.Perm.set_support_mul_subset`, `Equiv.Perm.set_support_symm_eq`, and the existing swap support/fixed-point finite lemmas instead of reproving the support calculations locally.
findings.issue: `isFinitelySupportedPerm_swap`, `IsFinitelySupportedPerm.mul`, and `IsFinitelySupportedPerm.symm` introduce parallel finite-support proofs for permutation support behavior already in Mathlib.

summary: The new exchangeable sigma-algebra itself is not a duplicate, but the finite-permutation support API re-proves existing Mathlib permutation-support facts instead of reusing them.
